title: Determine the exact value of R(B4, B4)
lean_gate_reason: Lean is not the shortest remaining path because the exact claim is already a rediscovery; formalization here would be archival rather than publication-sealing.
micro_paper_assessment: Fail for the micro-paper lane. The exact theorem R(B4,B4)=18 is already in prior art via the prime-power diagonal-book result, and the Paley-17 witness is known family machinery rather than a frontier closure.
strongest_honest_claim: The quadratic-residue coloring of K17 is a correct monochromatic-B4-free witness, but this lower-bound slice and the exact equality R(B4,B4)=18 are already known in the literature.
candidate_theorem_slice: Known slice: the Paley coloring on 17 vertices yields R(B4,B4) >= 18; combined with the published prime-power theorem this gives the already-known exact value R(B4,B4)=18.
